And on the right side, PSG’s Ibrahim Mbaye is another player tracked Barcelona, but there has been no encouragement to date the French champions are willing to sell, and Barcelona are focused on the left side this summer.

Barcelona need a list of alternative targets because it is unlikely Manchester United change their position on Rashford, at least not so far out from the summer window opening, but Rashford is believed to be optimistic he can stay in Spain and feels he remains Barcelona’s top left-sided target.

United remain keen to get Rashford's wage permanently off their books, and it is not thought a new manager will change this position.

Rashford was informed by Manchester United last summer he had no future at Old Trafford.

He now has just over two years left on his current deal and it would require a major U-turn from the club if they decide to reintegrate him into the first-team squad should Barcelona fail to make the signing permanent.

Football.title: Why there are TWO 5:30pm Premier League games this weekend with Arsenal and Chelsea in bizarre clash
Football.content:
Why there are TWO 5:30pm Premier League games this weekend with Arsenal and Chelsea in bizarre clash

Arsenal and Chelsea will both kick-off their Premier League matches at 5.30pm on Saturday in a unique fixture quirk.

The Saturday teatime slot traditionally holds just one Premier League match but fans will be treated to a double header this weekend.

5 Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta will hope to mastermind a win over his former side Credit: Getty

5 Liam Rosenior's Chelsea are back in action 72 hours after their defeat to PSG Credit: Getty

The Gunners continue their title charge when Everton head to the Emirates Stadium while Chelsea face a tough test in their hopes of a top-four finish at home to Newcastle.

Chelsea’s match with Newcastle was the original choice in the slot with Arsenal’s clash set to take place on Sunday.

But the Gunners’ progression in the Champions League saw the game brought forward by 24 hours.

Arsenal, who secured a late 1-1 in their round-of-16 first leg with Bayer Leverkusen on Wednesday, host the Bundesliga outfit next Tuesday, live on talkSPORT.

Tuesday was the only possible day to play the second leg due to north London rivals Tottenham hosting Atletico Madrid on Wednesday.

If the Premier League opted against moving Arsenal’s Premier League clash with Everton then Mikel Arteta’s men would have played their home matches against Everton and Bayer Leverkusen just 54 hours apart.

The Premier League aims to schedule at least a 72-hour gap between kick-off times to protect player welfare.

How to follow Arsenal vs Everton and Chelsea vs Newcastle

Both fixtures will take place at 5.30pm this Saturday.

Fans can follow the action on talkSPORT as part of Gameday Phone-in with Jamie O’Hara and Jason Cundy from 5.30pm.

5 The Gunners welcome Everton to north London hoping to extend their lead at the top of the table Credit: Getty

Tune in to talkSPORT through the website via the live stream or find us on DAB digital radio through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.

Download and listen to all our live commentaries via the talkSPORT app.

Visit talkSPORT BET for all the latest odds and bet boosts for every big event.

Subscribe to our talkSPORT YouTube channel for news, views and analysis plus our regular goals show and for more, 

Both matches will be broadcast live and simultaneously on Sky Sports.

5 Bukayo Saka's winner was enough to secure victory at Brighton last time out Credit: Getty

Arsenal vs Everton: Preview

Arsenal can extend their lead at the top of the Premier League table when they host Everton this weekend.

Victory over Brighton in their previous league fixture and Manchester City’s 2-2 draw with Nottingham Forest, handed the Gunners a seven-point lead at the top of the table.

And that could extend to ten points before City kick off against West Ham on Saturday evening with a win over David Moyes’ side.

But Arsenal could have their work cut out against an Everton side who are unbeaten on the road since December.

5 Chelsea' turn their attention back to domestic duties on Saturday

Chelsea vs Newcastle: Preview

Chelsea will be hoping to put their midweek Champions League defeat at Paris Saint-Germain behind them for the visit of the Magpies.

The Blues were beaten 5-2 at the Parc des Princes in a match that saw boss Liam Rosenior’s decision to start goalkeeper Filip Jorgensen over Robert Sanchez failing to pay off.

Chelsea sit fifth in the Premier League, four points adrift on fourth-placed Aston Villa in the battle for Champions League qualification.

Sandwiched in between the double header with PSG, Chelsea face a Newcastle side with one eye on their Champions League second leg against Barcelona next Wednesday.

The 12th-placed Magpies were pegged back in added time in midweek when Lamine Yamal converted a penalty to salvage a 1-1 draw.

The Champions League arguably presents Eddie Howe’s men with their best opportunity of qualifying for the competition again next season.

Football.title: Abbi Summers calls on Tottenham to forfeit Liverpool match and avoid more damage
Football.content:
Abbi Summers calls on Tottenham to forfeit Liverpool match and avoid more damage

Abbi Summers insists Tottenham should spare themselves the hassle of playing Liverpool on Sunday, and 'take the 3-0' forfeit.

Spurs head to Anfield in one of the bleakest spells in their history, currently on an 11-match winless run in the Premier League which has plunged them into a relegation battle.

3 Igor Tudor appears unlikely to remain as Tottenham boss beyond the March international break Credit: Getty

In a nightmare start to life in north London, boss Igor Tudor has lost all four of his matches since replacing Thomas Frank last month.

Things went from bad to worse on Tuesday, as Tottenham were beaten 5-2 by Atletico Madrid in the Champions League.

The horrendous run of form has already placed Tudor's future at the club in major doubt, and talkSPORT understands he has alienated a large portion of the dressing room with his treatment of goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky in the Atletico clash.

But the situation could get worse yet at the weekend, when his side head north with a real risk of dropping into the bottom three.

Sitting 16th in the table, Spurs are now just one point above the relegation zone, and could themselves in 18th place if they fail to beat Liverpool.

But history is not on the north Londoners' side here, who have won just once in their last 16 Premier League games against the Reds.

Meanwhile, Liverpool have lost just one of their last 31 home league games against Spurs, and are unbeaten in 14 since a 2-0 loss in May 2011.

'Give them the three points'

Recent results and history have not filled Tottenham fan Summers with confidence, so much so that she would rather her side not travel to Anfield at all on Sunday.

On the latest episode of talkSPORT's Inside Spurs, she said: “I honestly mean this wholeheartedly - I would happily forfeit this game.

Inside Spurs on talkSPORT talkSPORT does Tottenham like nobody else. Stay ahead of every tactical shift, manager sacking and transfer rumour by making talkSPORT's Inside Spurs show your weekly go to for all things Tottenham related. Subscribe now to ensure you never miss a beat of the action one episode at a time, sponsored by BetMGM. Download the app or visit BetMGM.co.uk. 18+gambleaware.org

"I'd take the 3-0. Just give them the three points."

Explaining her reasoning, Summers insisted that playing against Liverpool on Sunday will do Spurs more harm than good.

Tottenham could be without up to 11 first-team players at Anfield, including both starting centre-backs in Micky van de Ven - who is suspended - and Cristian Romero, after he suffered a clash of heads with Joao Palhinha against Atletico Madrid.

And the broadcaster, who was reduced to tears by her side's woes on a recent Inside Spurs episode, claimed Spurs would be better off not risking any further damage.

On forfeiting the game, Summers continued: "Realistically, firstly, it saves us the travel.

"It saves any injuries or any other suspensions.”

Nothing to smile about

Rounding off her bleak verdict, the Tottenham superfan tipped her team not only to lose, but to be thrashed by Liverpool.

3 Micky van de Ven will miss the Liverpool clash after being sent off in Tottenham's last match against Crystal Palace Credit: Getty

3 Tottenham are fighting for their lives at the bottom of the table

“If you know you're going to get beat, which we are, right? Knowing us, we probably will end up being embarrassed.

“[Mohamed] Salah has had a poor season. [Cody] Gakpo has had a poor season - they'll probably both score a hat-trick. So it could be like 6-1 or something like that.

“My thing is, take the 3-0. Take it, move on, keep it moving.”

Football.title: Why there is no 12:30pm Premier League game this Saturday as fans must wait for action
Football.content:
Why there is no 12:30pm Premier League game this Saturday as fans must wait for action

After a two week break from the Premier League, fans will have to wait a little longer on Saturday to get their football fix.

Usually, the first game each weekend is the 12:30pm kick off but there will be no early start this Saturday.

3 Man City are currently seven points behind Arsenal, with a game in hand

The first two games of matchweek 30 are scheduled for 3pm on Saturday, as the Premier League makes its return after a break for the FA Cup weekend.



Burnley will be hosting Bournemouth and Sunderland are set to take on Brighton.

But what is the reason for there being no early kick-off on Saturday? talkSPORT.com takes a look...

Why is there no 12:30pm game this weekend?

There was meant to be a 12:30pm start in the Premier League on Saturday, March 14.

However, there was a fixture change two weeks ago.

West Ham vs Manchester City was scheduled to take place in the early slot, with game live on TNT Sports.



But as Pep Guardiola's side played away in the Champions League against Real Madrid on Wednesday evening, the game was moved.

Man City released a statement on the enforced change, writing: "City travel to the London Stadium to face the Hammers on Saturday 14 March with the game now kicking off at 20:00 (UK).



"The match was moved to later in the day following the UEFA Champions League Round of 16 draw and the confirmation of our European schedule.

3 Arsenal will be hoping to extend their lead against Everton after equalising against Bayer Leverkusen midweek Credit: Getty



"We will face Real Madrid in the Spanish capital at 20:00 (UK) kick-off on Wednesday 11 March, with the second leg at the Etihad Stadium at 20:00 (UK) on Tuesday 17 March.

"TV viewers in the UK will be able to watch our match with West Ham on TNT Sports."

talkSPORT will also have live coverage from the London Stadium, in a massive game for both sides at opposite ends of the table.

Reshmin Chowdhury will be your host with commentary from Joe Shennan and former England striker Dean Ashton.

Tune in to talkSPORT through the website via the live stream or find us on DAB digital radio through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.



Download and listen to all our live commentaries via the talkSPORT app.

Visit talkSPORT BET for all the latest odds and bet boosts for every big event.

Subscribe to our talkSPORT YouTube channel for news, views and analysis plus our regular goals show and for more, 

Premier League fixtures this weekend Saturday, March 14 Burnley vs Bournemouth, 3pm

Sunderland vs Brighton, 3pm

Arsenal vs Everton, 5:30pm

Chelsea vs Newcastle United, 5:30pm

West Ham vs Man City, 8pm - live on talkSPORT Sunday, March 15 Crystal Palace vs Leeds, 2pm

Man United vs Aston Villa, 2pm

Nottingham Forest vs Fulham, 2pm

Liverpool vs Tottenham, 4:30pm Monday, March 16 Brentford vs Wolves,8pm - live on talkSPORT

3 Wolves are back in action in hopes to pull off 'The Great Escape'

There may not be an early kick-off on Saturday, but there is still a full set of Premier League fixtures coming up.



There are surprisingly two games at 5:30pm, as Arsenal host Everton and Chelsea play Newcastle.

Then, on Sunday, there are four fixtures to look forward to, as Crystal Palace take on Leeds, Manchester United host Aston Villa and Nottingham Forest take on Fulham at 2pm.

And as Tottenham fight for their Premier League survival, their game is live at 4:30pm from Anfield as they battle Liverpool.

On Monday, Wolves travel to face Brentford, with that game also live on talkSPORT with Adrian Durham, Sam Matterface and former England midfielder Danny Murphy.

Football.title: Chelsea have ‘unthinkable’ transfer fears put to rest as star signs new deal
Football.content:
Reece James has signed a new contract with Chelsea to put any fears of a summer exit to rest.

The Blues captain, who joined the club as a six-year-old, was previously out of contract in 2028.

2 James has made 35 appearances for Chelsea this season Credit: Getty

Chelsea have confirmed the 26-year-old has signed a new six-year deal which runs until 2032.



James said: "I am over the moon to have extended my contract - Chelsea means so much to me.

"I have always said I want my peak years to be here, and I truly believe we have everything it takes to build on our previous successes.

‘I’m excited for the future under this ownership, Sporting Directors, Coach and all the staff, and hopefully we will lift many more trophies together over the coming years."

Sporting directors Paul Winstanley and Laurence Stewart said: "On behalf of ownership and everyone connected to Chelsea, it is a proud moment for all of us to have Reece extend his contract.



"He is the leader of this team, both on and off the pitch. Reece knows what it means to wear this badge and, having committed his long-term future to Chelsea, we’re looking forward to continuing the journey and working towards further success together."

talkSPORT understands the Blues hierarchy expect a number of other contract extensions to follow - with Marc Cucurella and Levi Colwill thought to be top of their priority list.



James has been a star player for the Blues for many years after breaking through the academy.

The Englishman has started 19 Premier League games this season, registering two goals and four assists, appearing to put years of injury trouble behind him.



James has been regularly available for the first time since the 2021/22 campaign, playing a starring role across midfield and defence.

It is understood James' new deal aligns him with Chelsea's current incentivised wage structure.

He had been the only player at the club still on terms from the club's previous ownership.

Speaking on his journey with the club, last year he said: "It has been up and down along the way and it was tough to get into the first team and then stay there.

"But the journey has been an eventful and joyful one and I am so grateful to be in the position I am in now.



"It means everything to me, it's an amazing feeling – not only for me but also for my family."

The defender has been with the west London club for 19 years and the prospect of losing him in the summer started to feel real as talks over a new deal rumbled on.

Fear not, Chelsea fans

James is a big fan favourite among the Chelsea faithful with many not able to picture the team without him.

However, the Blues hierarchy would have been desperate to avoid letting James enter the final two years of his contract, and may have been forced to consider a sale to prevent him leaving for free in 2028.

The situation would have not have been unheard of after James' England teammate, Trent-Alexander Arnold, left boyhood club Liverpool for Real Madrid last summer at the end of his contract.



Ex-Chelsea defender Jason Cundy told fans prior to the contract extension that the new owners, BlueCo, would not allow that situation to manifest itself under the club's current financial model.

The co-host on our Sports Bar told Inside Chelsea: "Watch this space. He will be one of the highest-paid players at the club. They need to do something about that.

"We need to keep Reece James. We need to somehow find a way to get him to sign a new contract.

2 The Chelsea captain has won the Champions League, Conference League, FA Cups and most recently the Club World Cup Credit: Getty

"Look at what Trent did at Liverpool. If James gets into the last year of his contract, you can’t allow that. He could leave for nothing.



“Marc Guehi is worth three times what Man City just paid for him."

“One thing I understand from the new owners when they came in... is that they were astonished we let Antonio Rudiger and Andreas Christensen - worth more than £100m between them - walk on a free. They could not get their heads around that.

“I will be stunned and amazed if they allow Reece to have so much power without signing a new contract.

“It’s a fear. It’s a big concern. A football career is not long. Players want to know where they’re going.

“If Reece decides this project is not for him now, unfortunately, the club may well have to sell him in the summer. I don’t want to scaremonger or anything…"

Football.title: Disabled Aston Villa fan left Lille match in fear after seeing ‘blood all over the concourse’
Football.content:
Gabby Agbonlahor has urged authorities to investigate the treatment handed out to travelling English football fans in UEFA competitions.

It comes after French police clashed with Aston Villa fans prior to Thursday’s Europa League clash with Lille - and one fan told us they left after ten minutes out of fear, seeing blood all over the concourse.

3 French police clash with Aston Villa fans before their match against Lille Credit: AFP

The Premier League side claimed a 1-0 victory in their round-of-16 first leg thanks to Ollie Watkins’ second-half winner.

However, the result was overshadowed by shocking footage from outside the Stade Pierre-Mauroy.

A video circulating on social media shows police using batons as they stop supporters from entering the stadium.

Tear gas also appears to be sprayed towards them before a gate is slammed shut in front of the waiting Villa fans.

Meanwhile, it has been claimed that tasers were also used on the travelling supporters.

talkSPORT has contacted Aston Villa for comment.

It is not the first time English fans have reported heavy-handed tactics while entering French stadia.

Liverpool supporters were penned in against perimeter fences and stuck in a motorway underpass prior to the 2022 Champions League final at the Stade de France.

And more recently Reds’ fans suffered further difficulties entering the Stade Velodrome for their Champions League clash at Marseille in January.

In November 2023, Newcastle fans were attacked in a bar by a masked group on the eve of their match at Paris Saint-Germain.

And Steve, who attended the game on Thursday night, phoned talkSPORT to give Breakfast co-host Agbonlahor their eye-witness account.

Disabled fan leaves

He said: “What did we see? Villa fans of all ages and they said, oh we've just been tear gassed, we've just been tear gassed. So we're thinking, oh blimey, what are we going to walk into here?

“And then they kind of let us through the next phase and you go to the next part where there's another cage where all the food outlets are and literally there was loads of stewards being very, very heavy-handed.

“The police were in the corner by that part so I didn't see what was happening there but there were some Villa fans in a really bad way. There was a lot of blood all over the concourse.

“So I said to my mate Trevor, who's disabled, I said, what do you want to do?

"And he was like, I don't want to go in. We did for about 10 minutes and do you know what? The atmosphere, it just wasn't great.

3 Liverpool fans were poorly treated by police at the Stade de France before the 2022 Champions League final Credit: AFP

“So literally we, and there were a lot of people, again of all ages, just saying I'm done, I'm not going to bother.

“So literally we turned around and the kind of decision that you make then where you think, is it really worth it?

"And then we came out and we ended up watching the rest of the game in a bar. So yeah, it wasn't nice, it wasn't pleasant but unfortunately it's kind of what we come to expect whenever we go away."

Asked if the heavy-handed police approach had become a common feature when following Villa in Europe, Steve said: "Yeah, it is. Like Bruges, Feyenoord, Rotterdam, like literally they pen you in.

“And to be honest, honestly, I know it's easy to say but Villa fans are good as gold. They go, the crack is good, they have a good time.

“But literally it's almost as if you're kind of being wound up a little bit. You're kind of being intimidated and it's really, it's really not the one.”

3 Gabby Agbonlahor was appalled by he treatment of Aston Villa fans in Lille on Thursday evening

Unfair treatment

Former Aston Villa striker Agbonlahor voiced his annoyance at the treatment dished out to English fans on the continent and spoke of his own experience during his trip to PSG’s Parc des Princes last season.

“It doesn't surprise me,” Agbonlahor told talkSPORT. “I would have to say, I went to PSG away last season in the Champions League, and everyone was on edge before the game. There were some incidents in local bars. Villa fans were attacked.

“But the police did do a good job of sort of getting us all in and getting us all out safely. But I'm not surprised. And what annoys me is that we get a bad name, English fans, when they go abroad.

“But I'm sure the Villa fans, and I had friends that were over there, just want to get into the game. But the way they treat you, and it's not just Villa, a lot of other fan bases can say the same. They treat you terribly.

“But when we, as police over here, Fenerbahce, they trashed the City ground, Nottingham Forest Stadium, early on in the season.

“I bet they got goody bags on the way out. That's how nice we are over here to fans that cause carnage and rip seats out. The English fans don't do that abroad. But they get treated so badly.

“And there's got to be something done about it.”

The former Villa star called upon the authorities to look over the footage that has circulated online in recent hours as proof of the unfair treatment.

“Surely the footage that's been put out, that can be sent and complaints being made, because kids would have been there.

"I know people that take their kids to European away games. And young kids. Going through that experience is not nice.

“I was at the PSG game last season and I remember like, I was with my missus actually, it was her birthday and we were kept in for like probably an hour and a half and then like you say, different sections and you finally get out and it's like a cage and Villa fans have got their hands through the cage of like, let us out.

“Sort of like, you feel like a caged animal. And it's like, they don't get that treatment over here, do they?”

News.title: Woman, 18, who partied and went on ski trip after killing two teen pals in horror smash while speeding is jailed
News.content:
DEATH SMASH Woman, 18, who partied and went on ski trip after killing two teen pals in horror smash while speeding is jailed

A SPEEDING driver who partied and went on a skiing holiday after killing her two teen pals in a horror crash has been jailed.

Madeleine Lonsdale had only been driving for 12 weeks when she lost control on a bend and smashed into a tree at 78mph.





Harrison Carter, 18, and George Stephenson, 17, were both killed in the collision in the village of Marston, Lincolnshire.

Lincoln Crown Court heard following their deaths, Lonsdale partied “repeatedly” and choreographed TikToks.

The unremorseful driver also delayed her first court appearance for two weeks by going on a skiing trip.

Lonsdale has now been jailed for 14 months after pleading guilty to two counts of causing death by careless or inconsiderate driving.

The court was told Harrison and George were passengers in Lonsdale’s Kia Niro when they went to a petrol station in the early hours of June 20.

Lonsdale had raced another car at 100mph during the trip to buy snacks, alcohol and vapes.

Prosecutor Steven Taylor said: “She took a left turn down a rural country road and … down that road for a distance, evidently accelerated to over 70mph.



“In due course she failed to negotiate a right-hand bend further along the road.”

He added: “It appears she misjudged the distance to the first bend and failed to slow down at all on her approach.”

Lonsdale was travelling at 76mph seconds before she collided with the tree – causing the car to land on its side.

The unlit road was subject to a 60mph limit and a sign warned of the upcoming bend.

Harrison, who was in the front passenger seat, and George, who was sitting directly behind him, were both trapped in the car.



Lonsdale, who suffered a fractured wrist, approached a passing driver and asked if he could get the two friends out of the car.

Mr Taylor said: “He saw they were motionless and believed they were already deceased.”

The pair, who would have been rendered unconscious immediately due to their head injuries, were tragically declared dead at the scene.

Lonsdale, who passed a breath test, denied speeding but admitted the bend came up sooner than she was expecting.

Another car driven by a teenage friend was following Lonsdale’s vehicle but managed to avoid the collision, it was said.



Mr Taylor said Lonsdale and the other driver decided to “see how fast their respective vehicles could go” and “effectively raced each other” before the crash – briefly travelling at 100mph on the A1.

In a victim impact statement, Harrison’s mum Sarah Carter said her son wanted to pursue a career in law.

She added: “Harry was taken from us because Madeleine chose to speed.

“He will never be able to celebrate the amazing exam results that he achieved.”

Ms Carter also said it “adds insult to injury” that Lonsdale was “partying repeatedly” and “choreographing TikToks” while she planned her son’s funeral.



Victoria Stephenson, George’s mum , told the court: “We will never see him graduate or find a job after university.

“We didn’t get to celebrate his 18th birthday with him, instead we had his funeral.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

She said that when Lonsdale went on a skiing trip, delaying her first court appearance by two weeks, it “only added to the torment”.

As well as a jail term, Lonsdale was banned from driving for three years.

News.title: Heart-stopping moment young girl gets stuck in QUICKSAND on UK beach – before mum also gets trapped trying to save her
News.content:
THIS is the heart-stopping moment a young girl gets stuck in quicksand on a beach – and then her mum gets trapped trying to save her.

Shocking CCTV video captures the youngster playing in the sand at 5:20pm, near the Three Shells Lagoon, in Southend, Essex, before she suddenly sinks into the ground.





Moments later, her mum can be seen rushing to her daughter’s aid, only to sink in the quicksand and become stuck just metres away from her.

The scared duo began shouting for help during the incident on March 10, before four men from the nearby Adventure Island theme park, as well as a lorry driver, came to their rescue.

Video shows the men pulling both the mum and daughter out of the sand with a rope.



It is believed the sand the duo became stuck in was placed there by Southend-on-Sea City Council.

In a post on Facebook, a spokesperson for Adventure Island said: “Yesterday at around 5:20pm, one of our managers spotted a mother and her young daughter stranded in the Three Shells Lagoon, waist-deep and stuck in wet sand that had recently been placed there by the council, with the tide coming in.

“He immediately alerted members of our workshop team, who quickly brought ropes to secure around them and carefully pull them to safety.

“Drawing on their training and expertise, the team acted quickly and calmly to rescue them, while another member of the workshop team contacted the Coastguard.



“Thankfully this happened while our team were still on site at the end of the day, allowing staff to spot the situation and step in when it mattered most.

“A huge well done to our team who jumped straight into action and safely rescued them.

“We hope the mother and daughter are both safe and well after the incident.

What is quicksand? Quicksand can become deadly without warning, and it can strike at British beaches. Quicksand forms when an excessive amount of water flows through sand reducing the friction between the particles. The result is a mushy mixture of sand that cannot support weight. Contrary to myth quicksand does not suck people downwards but movement can cause a person to sink further. Expert advice is not to struggle, make slow movements to bring yourself to the surface and then lie back and float to a safe level. How to keep your family safe at the beach 1. Remember it is easy to get into trouble in the water

Be aware of the dangers and do not take risks. Go with friends and look out for one another, and always have your phone on you at the beach. 2. Visit beaches with lifeguards and swim between the red and yellow flags 3. Consider your ability and the conditions

Make sure you know your limits – remember, swimming in the sea is very different to swimming in a pool. 4. Acclimatise to the temperature

The British sea is cold, so take time to get used to the temperature. 5. Have someone watch you

Have someone keep an eye on you from the beach, and make sure they have a means of calling for help if something goes wrong. 6. If you end up in difficulties at sea

Relax, float on your back to catch your breath and try to grab something that will help you float. Keep calm and call for help or swim for safety if you are able. 7. If you see someone else in trouble

Call 999 or 112 and ask for the coastguard. If you have something that floats or they can hold on to, throw it to them. Do not go into the water yourself – many people drown trying to save others. Source: RNLI For more information go to: respectthewater.com

“If they see this post, we’d love them to get in touch with us.”

Philip Miller MBE, Executive Chairman of Stockvale Group, thanked the team for their efforts.



He said: “They recognised that two people were in real difficulty and worked together, with real calmness and professionalism, to bring them safely out while the Coastguard were contacted.

The lagoon is currently fenced off with signs warning visitors over the “deep wet stand”.

A Southend Council spokesperson said: “We are aware of this incident and are taking it very seriously.

“Our engineers have already been on site to install fencing and signage around the lagoon.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“No injuries were reported and we will keep the signage on site advising caution due to deep, wet sand until we are confident that the lagoon is safe.



“We will consider any other urgent measures which may be necessary following the next low-tide inspection.”

News.title: Iranian rank-&-file troops deserting in droves after they’re given just 10 bullets per soldier & wounded are left to die
News.content:
IRANIAN troops are deserting in their droves after being handed just 10 bullets each – while those injured in US-Israeli airstrikes are being left to die, sources have claimed.

As the Iran war enters its third week, frontline units have been left struggling with limited ammunition and inadequate basic supplies – such as drinking water and food.





Sources cited by Iran International described the conditions as “harsh” – and warned that some soldiers perceive them as neglect by commanders.

“In one example cited by the sources, some units were issued only 20 bullets for every two Artesh [regular Army] soldiers, leaving troops with little capacity to respond to potential attacks,” the report said.

Intelligence firm Flashpoint warned of “tensions” between the Army and the Iran‘s Revolutionary Guards as a result – pointing to the “fragility” of the Iranian military.



One of the sharpest points of friction appears to involve medical support for wounded soldiers.

Sources said that regular army units are suffering significant casualties – but that IRGC personnel have refused to transport injured army soldiers to hospitals despite having access to medical facilities.

It comes as…

Revolutionary Guards officials are understood to have rejected repeated army requests for assistance, citing shortages of ambulances and blood supplies.



“[This friction] includes refusal to transport wounded army soldiers and suggests the Iranian military system is under unsustainable internal stress,” Flashpoint said in a briefing.

That strain, however, is reportedly not limited to Artesh – The Islamic Republic of Iran Army.

IRGC – founded in the early days of the 1979 Islamic Revolution by Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeiniseen – which are seen as the spine of the regime, are reportedly facing similar conditions.

Even within IRGC missile units – traditionally the best resourced parts of Iran’s military – there have been reports of communications equipment failures and shortages of food and other basic supplies.

The US-Israeli air campaign has struck thousands of sites across Iran, most belonging to the military and the IRGC.



Earlier today, the Israeli Defence Forces (IDF) said that more than 6,000 IRGC operatives, including senior commanders and senior regime officials, have been killed by IDF fire, but the figure has not been independently confirmed.

In addition to killing operatives, the IDF has been carrying out widespread strikes on defence industries in a variety of fields.

Thousands of civilian sites have also been damaged – including hospitals, schools, universities and homes, the Iranian Red Crescent confirmed.

A large explosion also rocked the Ferdowsi Square area of Tehran midday.

Thousands had gathered for the annual Al Quds day rally organised by the government in support of Palestinians, in which they chanted “death to Israel” and “death to America”.



Israel had earlier warned that it would target the area in the capital, but few Iranians would have seen it, as authorities have almost completely shut down the internet since the start of the war.

Footage from the scene showed people chanting “God is greatest” as smoke rose in the area.

This comes as Donald Trump said the US was going to be hitting Iran “very hard over the next week” after previously declaring that the war is now “complete”.

In an interview with Fox News, he said the war would end “when I feel it in my bones”.

He later wrote on Truth Social: “Iran’s Navy is gone, their Air Force is no longer, missiles, drones and everything else are being decimated, and their leaders have been wiped from the face of the earth.



“We have unparalleled firepower, unlimited ammunition, and plenty of time.

“Watch what happens to these deranged scumbags today.”

Axios also cited three officials from the G7, which includes the UK, as saying that he had told leaders in a virtual meeting on Wednesday that Iran was “about to surrender.”

“I got rid of a cancer that was threatening us all,” the US President had told them.

Trump further addressed the ongoing tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, saying that the US would escort vessels through the key shipping lane.



Asked about helping oil tankers pass through the strait, he said: “We would do it if we needed to.

“But, you know, hopefully things are going to go very well. We’re going to see what happens.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Iran has continued to launch widespread daily missile and drone attacks on Israel and neighbouring Gulf states, and has effectively shut the lane to any oil.

The newly-elected Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei on Thursday vowed to continue the strikes and keep the strait closed in his first public statement since succeeding his father.

News.title: Girl, 7, drowned at summer camp after being given wrong-coloured wristband
News.content:
LAKE TRAGEDY Girl, 7, drowned at summer camp after being given wrong-coloured wristband

SIX people are facing manslaughter charges after a girl, 7, drowned at a church summer camp in a horror wristband mix-up.

Anisa Murati’s body was found at the bottom of a lake in Caraglio, Italy, after she was given the wrong-coloured wristband.





She was wearing an orange wristband which indicated she was confident in the water.

She allegedly should have been wearing green for low-skilled swimmers.

Emergency services were called July 17, 2024, after panicked camp staff could only find Anisa’s pink sandals at the edge of the bio-lake.

She was reportedly last seen around 3.30pm, but the search only began an hour later.

Divers later recovered her body around 6pm from the bottom of the 6’5″ft deep lake.

Now, almost two years later, public prosecutors in nearby Cuneo have requested that six people face trial for manslaughter.

Among those named are engineer Stefano Ferrari and architect Graziano Viale, who signed off on AcquaViva bio-park, and its manager, Roberto Manzi.

The others are parish priest Fabrizio Della Bella, who organised the summer camp, and the two young animators responsible for supervising the children.



Investigators say all six displayed “negligence and incompetence” and claim the biopark suffered from structural, design, and safety deficiencies.

By wearing the wrong wristband, it allegedly allowed her to approach the bio-lake – which had murky waters and a slippery bed – without flotation armbands.

Acqua Viva Biopark manager Manzi reportedly called Della Bella to warn him that the animators were not supervising the children properly, but prosecutors say he failed to intervene.

Only two animators were present and when the alarm was raised, just one was actually on duty, even though four lifeguards were legally required.

Prosecutors also allege that Manzi failed to provide basic safety measures, such as a floating barrier separating the shallow and deep areas of the lake.



Ferrari and Viale are accused of signing off on the completed bio-park works even though they allegedly did not conform to the approved project.

Anisa was the daughter of Albanian parents, Jetmir and Rozafe, who live in Demonte with two other children.

The couple had already lost another daughter to illness in 2019 while living in Prazzo.

At the time, Anisa’s headteacher, Diego Deidda, described her as “a bright, cheerful child, friendly and good at school”.

Jetmir, then 37, called Anisa “my life” and recalled: “My little girl wore glasses. She always kept them in her bag because I taught her to take them off before going in the water so they wouldn’t break.



“We found them in her bag. That means she went into the water on purpose – she didn’t just fall in by accident.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Why didn’t anyone see her? A child moves and struggles in the water – you would notice.”

The case continues.

News.title: Major bin day change announced for 32,000 UK households – and will kick in next month
News.content:
THOUSANDS of households are set to face major shake-ups to their rubbish collections next month.

Around 32,000 households in Weymouth, Portland, Chickerell and Osmington will see their collection day altered, or at least their bin collection times adjusted.



Dorset Council says letters will be sent out to everyone affected, explaining exactly what the changes mean for each property.



The new system kicks off on Monday, April 13, following a review of waste routes from the Weymouth depot.

Some residents will simply have a new day for collections, while others might see no change. In certain cases, the time of day bins are emptied could be different too.

The council says the shake-up is designed to balance routes, making them easier to manage and more resilient as the local population grows.

Cllr Jon Andrews, Dorset Council’s Cabinet Member for Place Services. said; “These changes will help keep our rounds efficient and reliable.”

He urged residents to watch out for their letters. “Everything you need to know will be in it. Our teams are ready to answer questions and make the transition as smooth as possible,” he added.

The council has been using modern route-planning tools and local data to make sure the collections run on time without needing extra trucks or staff.

During the switch, some homes may get an extra collection, or be allowed a little extra side waste, depending on their situation.

Each letter will outline the new collection day, the start date, and any temporary arrangements during the transition.



If you don’t get a letter, there’s no need to worry, this just means your bin schedule will stay the same.

Residents are being reminded to put their bins out as usual until they receive confirmation of any change.

The council says the redesign has been tested to reflect road layouts, access, and how long it takes to service each street, ensuring the service remains reliable for years to come.

News.title: Shocking moment on-duty police officer ROMPS next to his patrol car as CCTV of brazen tryst goes viral
News.content:
THIS is the scandalous moment CCTV catches a cop mid-romp with a civilian whilst on duty.

The randy young officer arranged to meet with the woman near the Cañuelas Golf Club in Buenos Aires, Argentina and proceeded to get hot and heavy on a patch of grass beside his patrol car.





Little did they know, the golf course security cameras were filming every second.

Shocking footage of the saucy rendezvous went viral online and triggered an Internal Affairs investigation.

The 22-year-old cop was promptly dismissed over his inappropriate conduct.



A police spokesperson said: “He stopped at the location for about 10 minutes with a civilian woman he arranged to meet.

“Patrol cars are equipped with a device that alerts the authorities when they stop for over half an hour, that’s why there wasn’t an alert in this case.

“We only found out when the images went viral.”

He is the second police officer to be fired in recent months.



In December, another 22-year-old cop from Cañuelas was dismissed for firing a shotgun into the air to celebrate Christmas.

He posted videos of himself firing four shots whilst dressed in civilian clothes, sparking public outcry.

News.title: OnlyFans model left for dead after ‘Dubai Porta Potty party’ horror makes incredible comeback as she poses in glam pics
News.content:
THE OnlyFans model left for dead after a suspected “Dubai Porta Potty party” has made a remarkable comeback.

Last year, Maria Kovalchuk, 21, was feared dead after she was discovered unconscious on a roadside in Dubai, suffering from catastrophic injuries.





The 21-year-old Ukrainian had her hair cut off with a knife and was scalped, while also suffering a broken spine and leg.

At the time, there were fears she had been a victim of one of the notorious “Porta Potty” parties.

These shadowy gatherings involve women, often aspiring models or influencers, who are lured to various settings with the promise of huge payments and then forced to perform degrading and violent sexual acts involving bodily fluids.

Now, against the odds, Maria has returned to the limelight.

She recently revealed she has travelled to Paris to study makeup, attending courses that include masterclasses from Chanel and Bobbi Brown.

Her recovery has been long and gruelling.

Maria spent months in Norway, where she lives in exile from her war-torn homeland of Ukraine, undergoing intensive treatment and rehabilitation.



In total, she endured ten major operations and was initially confined to a wheelchair.

A year on, she is back on her feet.

“At first, I felt literally shattered, like a broken doll, and I could not imagine my future life at all,” Maria told her followers.

“I kept wondering how I would live with all these injuries, with his scar, disfigured… how I would somehow go on with my life.

“But over time, my perception of the world began to change.



“I started paying attention to my inner self and realised that what matters most is your inner state – what you learn and what you feel.

“Of course, there were difficulties with this, too.

“But I managed thanks to the support of my loved ones and my friends.”

Maria claims she was tortured by wealthy Russians during a three-day party in Dubai – contradicting earlier speculation that “sheikhs” had been responsible for her injuries.

The Russians have denied abusing her, and no one has ever been brought to justice over the injuries that almost killed her.



While she has almost no memory of the alleged torture, Maria said she missed her flight to Thailand while staying at the luxury Five Jumeirah Village hotel.

She then said a 19-year-old man offered her a place to stay, claiming his father could fly her out on a private jet.

However, Maria alleges that once she went with him, the man – along with several others – instead started torturing her.

CCTV footage that could have shown what happened was allegedly erased after three months, Maria claims, leaving no video evidence.

“The police waited until the cameras were automatically wiped… So now there’s no evidence,” she said.



According to her mother, Anna, her daughter underwent “three operations on one leg, three on the other, two on her spine, and one on her shoulder blade”.

The family has also revealed that Maria’s medical bills were fully covered by Dubai police – further fuelling speculation online about a possible cover-up.

Anna says she was threatened after speaking publicly about the case.

After her daughter went public, she received a disappearing message on her phone.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We’ll find you even in Norway,” it warned.



Dubai police claimed to have detained the accused young men – briefly – but released them the following day, with the case reportedly closed.

News.title: Horror moment hot air balloon CRASHES into building causing woman to be thrown from basket & fatally plunge onto rooftop
News.content:
THIS is the moment a hot air balloon crashes into a building as woman is thrown from the basket in a horror fatal plunge.

Champion hot air balloon pilot Jagoda Gancarek, 28, was killed in the disaster ride in Poland on Monday morning.





The white, green and yellow hot air balloon can be seen flying dangerously low over a block of flats.

The balloon crashes its way to street level.

Deflated, it is tangled in a tree and draped over a bus.



Jagoda and two other women were on board when the balloon reportedly hit an apartment block.

Upon impact, Jagoda spilled from the basket and fell onto the rooftop.

When paramedics arrived by helicopter, they were unable to save her despite frantic resuscitation efforts.

The balloon continued to crash land with the two other women still clinging to the basket.



They later miraculously stepped out without serious injury after making an emergency landing on the ground in the centre of Zielona Góra, western Poland.

The three women had been flying for an hour and a half from Lubuskie Region Aero Club when they got into trouble.

Investigations are now underway by Polish police and the country’s State Commission for Aircraft Accident Investigation.

Police spokesperson Małgorzata Stanisławska said: “As a result of the accident, a 28-year-old woman died after falling from the basket onto the roof of one of the buildings.



“Unfortunately, despite resuscitation efforts, she could not be saved.”

The basket lay on a street next to a bus, with the torn Czech-made balloon tangled in the branches of a tree above.

Jagoda was an instructor who spoke about the “silence and calm” on balloon flights.



“You’re left alone with your thoughts. It’s an incredible feeling,” she previously said.

“The adrenaline is there, of course, but above all, you just focus on getting things done.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Last year, she was crowned Polish Women’s Champion at the 11th Women’s Ballooning Championships in Nałęczów.

The club said in a statement: “We will remember Jagoda as a warm and always smiling person, full of passion and a love for flying.”

News.title: Boy, 3, killed in car park crash pictured for first time
News.content:
A toddler who tragically died after being struck by a car in a shopping centre car park has been pictured for the first time.

Noah Sikora, who has been described as a "source of endless joy to his family," sadly died following the horror incident at Charlestown Shopping Centre in Finglas, Dublin, on Wednesday, March 11.

This is a breaking news story.

News.title: Palace 'embarrassed' by Andrew hecklers as Royal Family left with tough option
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Palace 'embarrassed' by Andrew hecklers as Royal Family left with tough option

On Commonwealth Day, the senior royals were confronted with furious protesters outside Westminster Abbey - and one royal expert believes there is only one way to deal with the anti-monarchist disruption

View 4 Images The King arrived to Westminster Abbey with protestors in the background (Image: Ian Vogler / Daily Mirror)

King Charles and a host of senior royals were confronted with a large group of furious protesters earlier this week, as members of the anti-monarchy group Republic gathered outside Westminster Abbey ahead of the Commonwealth Day service. The monarch, as well as the Prince and Princess of Wales and Princess Anne, were forced to ignore their chants as they entered the London church for the annual service.

‌



The group held large bright yellow placards and chanted “Abolish the monarchy” and “Down with the Crown” as the royals entered the Abbey. They also held signs that read “Charles, what did you know?” in relation to Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s involvement with Jeffrey Epstein.

‌



Kate Middleton gives health update and reveals what she's cut back since cancer diagnosis READ MORE :

View 4 Images William and Kate arrived for the Commonwealth Day service as protestors demonstrate in the background (Image: AP)

‌



Some of the signs featured images of the disgraced royal that emerged when millions of documents from the Epstein files were released, as the protesters demanded answers from the King about his younger brother’s indiscretions.

The Republic group have made themselves known at dozens of royal events over the years, including Trooping the Colour and Charles’ coronation, with the size of their demonstrations slowly increasing as the Royal Family grapples with the turbulent time.

Former BBC royal correspondent Jennie Bond believes that while the protesters are no doubt distracting from the royals’ duties, there is very little the Firm can do other than proceed business as usual.

‌



View 4 Images The demonstrators from anti-monarchy group Republic (Image: Ian Vogler / Daily Mirror)

Jennie told the Mirror : “There’s no denying that the Republican movement is making more of a splash – and more of a noise – these days. It’s obviously an embarrassment for the Royal Family and, for the first time, I think the movement is beginning to do some damage to the monarchy’s popularity. But the royals really have no choice but to ignore the shouting and carry on.”

Jennie explained that the royals are no strangers to protesters, dodging anti-monarchy sentiments at many events over the years, but have learned to drown out the noise and focus on the royal fans who show up for them instead.

‌



“I always think it’s a bit tough on the royals to be the targets of such angry protests,” the royal expert said. “They didn’t ask to be born into the jobs they’ve inherited and, in my opinion, they’re trying to make the best of it. One thing I’m sure they’ve learnt during their years of public duty is how to develop a thick skin.”

View 4 Images Jennie Bond believes the Royal Family simply have to continue as usual and ignore the protesters' calls (Image: POOL/AFP via Getty Images)

“So I think they’re quite right simply to carry on with their engagements, interact with the many people who are only too delighted to see them and try to blank out the sound of the protesters.”

‌



It seems the Prince and Princess of Wales were the blueprint for carrying on with their royal duties despite the protesters on Commonwealth Day, as body language expert Judi James told the Mirror that William and Kate were the picture of confidence as they entered the Abbey.

Judi said: "Studiously ignoring the huge bank of banners and posters advertising anti-monarchy sentiments, William and Kate appeared to be showing non-verbal signals of peak confidence on their arrival with what looked like a bid to dampen down all the dramas that have plagued the royals at this event, both historically and currently."

Article continues below

With Prince William and Princess Kate displaying poise and royal perfection at the annual event, Judi admitted the same could not be said for King Charles, who seemed "less assured" than his son as his "anxious" behaviours were on show as he dodged the furious calls of the protesters.

"Charles looked rather less assured than his son here,” Judi said. “Although his smile suggested he was delighted to attend, he performed four acts of auto-contact in the short walk from the gates to the abbey entrance, pushing his left hand deep into his jacket pocket and out again twice and straightening his tie before patting his pocket in what looked like self-comfort, self-soothing rituals prompted by inner anxiety."

News.title: Prince Harry 'will have to face questions over Epstein scandal but has reason for silence'
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Prince Harry 'will have to face questions over Epstein scandal but has reason for silence'

As Prince Harry and Meghan Markle prepare for their trip to Australia next month, they may need to prepare themselves for questions about the Jeffrey Epstein scandal

View 4 Images Harry may face questions about his uncle, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, and his involvement with Jeffrey Epstein

While Prince William and King Charles have both released strongly-worded statements about the Jeffrey Epstein scandal and Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, Prince Harry has yet to say anything publicly.

‌



But with plans underway for him and Meghan Markle to visit Australia next month for a series of "private, business and philanthropic engagements", it has raised questions about if - or when - that might change.

‌



According to PR expert and talent manager Dermot McNamara, the California-based couple, who stepped back as working royals in 2020, have a good reason for keeping quiet on the topic - but are in a very tricky spot.

‌



Kate Middleton gives health update and reveals what she's cut back since cancer diagnosis READ MORE :

Palace insider voices 'serious' concern on King Charles and Kate heckling at major event READ MORE :

View 4 Images The crisis involving Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor may be inescapable for the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, an expert says (Image: PA)

"The whole world is speculating on his family in some of the most awful context regarding Epstein," he tells the Mirror. "Harry wasn’t implicated but he’s probably still going to have to answer questions about it all.

"Whilst he probably doesn’t want to comment and be dragged into the Andrew-Epstein situation, he also doesn’t want to be seen as not commenting, so he’s in a very difficult position.

‌



“He probably knows it would be absolutely reckless to say anything, so he’s likely thinking the best thing to do is say nothing at all, while also letting his brother and father know that he is there, and they can support the family, but not to his own detriment."

Buckingham Palace issued a statement from King Charles last month, which read: "The King has made clear, in words and through unprecedented actions, his profound concern at allegations which continue to come to light in respect of Mr Mountbatten-Windsor's conduct."

Prince William and Kate Middleton, who were reportedly the driving force behind Andrew being stripped of his titles and honours last year, also said they were "deeply concerned" and "focused on the victims" after fresh information emerged in documents released from the Epstein files.

‌



View 4 Images Harry and Meghan previously visited Australia for their first official royal tour as a married couple in 2018 (Image: Getty Images)

The statement was released by Kensington Palace a few days before William's recent royal tour to Saudi Arabia, but failed to prevent him being questioned in public about his uncle. During a visit a youth football training session, a reporter twice shouted out to him: "Sir, to what extent do you think the Royal Family have done enough around the Andrew-Epstein issue?", although the Prince of Wales appeared not to hear, and did not respond.

With the scandal showing no signs of disappearing any time soon, Dermot says Harry and Meghan, 44, may also face similar questions when out and about in Oz next month.

‌



The pair, who are expected to travel without Prince Archie and Princess Lilibet, aged six and four, are keeping specific details about the trip under wraps, but it emerged last week that one of Meghan's engagements will be guest speaker at a ticketed gala dinner at a women-only retreat.

Prince Harry, 41, is also lined up to be the keynote speaker at the InterEdge Psychosocial Safety Summit in Melbourne, which will involve discussions about creating "mentally healthy, safe, and high-performing workplaces" according to the organisers.

View 4 Images Meghan Markle (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

‌



While Harry has been outspoken about some of his own experiences in the family, Dermot says, he is less likely to become embroiled in the situation regarding his uncle, who has denied any wrongdoing.

“He’s been honest in the past about his own experiences in the family in Spare, for example, but the current crisis involves other people and is about a highly sensitive issue which needs to be treated with caution and care," Dermot adds. “I think Harry was justified in talking about his own experiences, I’m sure there was a lot he left unsaid, as well as what he said publicly. In terms of he and William’s relationship, there has always been a power imbalance there, because William is next in line for the throne, and Harry is the spare.

"I think Harry’s actions may have recalibrated their relationship, even though it has been embarrassing for William at times. Sometimes the only way to recalibrate an imbalance is to slightly stand up to what's going on in a situation and say how you feel about it. "

Article continues below

News.title: American living in the UK stunned as he receives 'highest honour possible'
News.content:
American living in the UK stunned as he receives 'highest honour possible'

"Something amazing has happened as an American in the UK... Something that has taken years of hard work, trying to fit in, working on understanding of such a wonderful, beautiful culture. I've been given the highest honour the British have to give."

View Image People loved the heartwarming story shared by the American man living in the UK (stock image) (Image: Getty Images)

Uprooting your life and moving to an entirely different country can be daunting, and it's understandable to feel like an outsider in a new culture. But one American man in the UK has spoken of his pride at receiving what he described as "the highest honour possible" from his British in-laws.

‌



The man, who posts on social media as yankeedoodleintheuk, explained the heartwarming story on Instagram. In the video caption he wrote: "Something amazing has happened as an American in the UK... Something that has taken years of hard work, trying to fit in, working on understanding of such a wonderful, beautiful culture. I've been given the highest honour the British have to give."

‌



The clip also displayed the caption: "The British gave me the highest honour possible... and my life will never be the same." Opening the video, he said: "Something beautiful just happened to me the other day and I think it finally means I've been accepted into British society.

‌



"Nothing too dramatic, nothing too out of this world, just something simple. Something that made me feel decent.

"It was my mother-in-law's birthday party the other day, and we wanted to celebrate with the most British cake possible - the Colin the Caterpillar cake."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Marks and Spencer's legendary Colin the Caterpillar cake is a fixture of celebrations up and down the UK. Described by the retailer as "the original and best", M&S first started selling the cake in 1990. It's made up of large chocolate sponge roll with chocolate buttercream, covered in thick milk chocolate and chocolate beans - completed by the all-important white chocolate feet and face.

The man continued: "I couldn't go to the party because our nephews have chicken pox and I've never had them before. [I was] wishing I was there having some of the cake, so I asked my wife 'can you just bring me a slice back home?'

"So hours, hours go by and she returns home and says 'I've brought you back something, a surprise'. She opened up the tub and it wasn't just a slice or a slice of his belly.

‌



"It was his head! It was his lovely head!

"At that moment I was just like 'oh my God, Colin the Caterpillar's head!' It's the piece I've never had before, it's the relic, it's the Holy Grail, it's something beautiful.

"I took it in my hands and looked at it and like a little tear came down my cheek, and I thought 'this is my moment, my family fully loves me. I've got Colin the Caterpillar's head'."

Article continues below

People in the comments loved the story. One said: "They really love you." Another wrote: "You have arrived brotha!"

Someone else said: "You are the son she didn’t give birth to or adopted! You are loved, respected and valued!"

And another wrote: "Welcome, now just don't eat a Terry's chocolate orange like an apple and you're all good."

News.title: Double tragedy as teen finds girlfriend's body - and dies two weeks later
News.content:
Double tragedy as teen finds girlfriend's body - and dies two weeks later

Tom Dingle, 19, was found dead in woods a few miles from his home on Christmas Day, weeks after discovering girlfriend Natasha Braithwaite dead in her Lancaster University halls

View 3 Images Tom Dingle, 19, was found dead weeks after discovering his girlfriend's body (Image: LancsLive)

A teenager who was found dead on Christmas Day had discovered his girlfriend's body in her bedroom just weeks beforehand, an inquest has heard.

‌



Tom Dingle, from Appley Bridge in West Lancashire, was reported missing on December 13 last year. Lancashire Police launched a search operation following his disappearance before his body was discovered by a dog walker in woodland in the nearby village of Dalton on December 25.

‌



An inquest heard this week how the 19-year-old found his girlfriend dead on November 20, just weeks prior to his disappearance.

‌



Natasha Braithwaite, originally from Lincoln, had been a first-year undergraduate fine art student at Lancaster University, with the 19-year-old only weeks into her course when she was found in her room at Bowland College, Lancs Live reports.

Friends had described Natasha as a "sweet, artistic and talented girl".

View 3 Images Natasha Braithwaite was found dead in her halls of residence in November last year (Image: MEN Media)

‌



Family rally round after police sergeant mum-of-two found dead by husband READ MORE :

Premium Bonds account change warning over 'fast-approaching' deadline READ MORE :

During an inquest on March 10 into her death at Preston Crown Court, it emerged that the night before her death, Tom, who also studied at the university, had arranged to see her but fell asleep.

He awoke and tried to ring Natasha but when he couldn't reach her he went round to her halls of residence. He managed to rouse some of her flatmates and they gained access to her room, where they found her unresponsive in bed.

Paramedics rushed to the scene, but Natasha was pronounced dead upon arrival. The inquest was told she had been grappling with post-traumatic stress following an incident while in Abu Dhabi the previous December.

‌



Natasha had been taking drugs, and toxicology reports showed she had lethal levels of morphine in her body when she died.

View 3 Images Tom's family described him as an 'incredibly intelligent and caring young man' (Image: Lancashire Police)

Giving a summary prepared by a clinical scientist who analysed the findings, Assistant Coroner Sara Sutherland said: "The concentration of total morphine; that is morphine and its metabolites, is within the range encountered in fatalities attributed to the use of morphine. The compound taken in this case is likely to be heroin."

‌



In a family statement read by the coroner, Natasha was said to have appeared "excited" about starting university. She had been making future plans, including compiling her Christmas list, and had recently begun a relationship with Tom, with friend describing the pair as "inseparable".

Tom gave a statement to officers about Natasha weeks before he died, explaining how the couple had crossed paths at a fresher's night and characterised her as "a very kind girl who was extroverted".

Detective Constable Kirsten Bell said paraphernalia discovered in her room was thought to be connected to heroin use.

‌



The coroner delivered a verdict of a drug-related death and stated: "I have no doubt that Natasha was well-liked, extremely well-loved and would have been successful at uni. It is absolutely tragic."

In an online book of condolence set up by Lancaster University after Natasha's death, a flatmate said: "I truly don't think I've ever met a soul as beautiful, gentle, and caring as hers. She always greeted me with the warmest smile and I will forever cherish the little moments we shared."

A childhood mate penned: "Natasha was such a huge part of my early life. Her friendship and the bond we had truly shaped my childhood and who I am today. She is a true friend and a beautiful human-being, inside and out. The memories I have of her are a credit to her unique, bubbly and warm character."

‌



An inquest into Tom's death began in January, with a conclusive inquest set to take place at the same court on April 9.

In a written tribute, Tom's family said: "Tom was an incredibly intelligent and caring young man who had his whole life ahead of him. Tom's death has left a hole in our hearts which will never be filled. Christmas, and life as a whole, will never be the same without our Tom."

Bowland College, where Tom was studying at Lancaster University, also paid tribute to him on social media. A spokesperson said: "We are deeply saddened to share that a tragic update has been provided by Lancashire Constabulary in relation to the search for the missing Bowland College first year student Tom Dingle.

Article continues below

"His death will be a tremendous loss to friends and staff members who had the privilege to get to know him during his time at the University, and the Bowland community are with those affected by this sudden loss."

*If you're struggling and need to talk, the Samaritans operate a free helpline open 24/7 on 116 123. Alternatively, you can email jo@samaritans.org or visit their site to find your local branch.

News.title: Keir Starmer hits back as US eases Russian sanctions amid oil price spike
News.content:
Keir Starmer hits back as US eases Russian sanctions amid oil price spike

View 2 Images Keir Starmer's spokesman said allies must maintain the pressure on Russia (Image: Getty Images)

Downing Street has hit back at the US decision to loosen sanctions on Russian oil as Yvette Cooper accused the Kremlin and Iran of attempting to "hijack the global economy".

‌



Oil prices have rocketed to around $100 a barrel as energy markets were rocked by Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz - a crucial shipping lane. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent scrambled to curb the economic fallout by allowing temporary authorisation for countries to buy Russian oil already loaded on vessels at sea

‌



But Keir Starmer's spokesman said allies must maintain pressure on Russia and its war chest amid fears the Kremlin will exploit this opportunity to raise funds for its war on Ukraine. It marks another dividing line between Britain and the US after Mr Starmer refused to allow UK military bases to be used in initial strikes against Iran.

‌



View 2 Images Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper during a visit to a British military base on the edge of Riyadh in Saudi Arabia (Image: PA)

Asked if Mr Starmer was disappointed by the US move on sanctions, the PM's official spokesman said: "It's obviously a decision for the US, but our position is clear. All partners should maintain pressure on Russia and its war chest.

"Our support for Ukraine is degrading Russia's ability to wage war around the globe, both militarily and financially. The best way to continue to stop Russia supporting hostile actors is to continue on collective pressure and end the war in Ukraine."

‌



Asked to rule out the UK weakening its sanctions regime, the PM's spokesman said: "Our sanctions remain, there's no question about that. We remain committed to exerting that maximum economic pressure."

Earlier, Energy Minister Michael Shanks said: "This is an absolutely critical moment in the Russian aggression against Ukraine and sanctions are important.

"What we absolutely can't have is Putin sitting in the Kremlin thinking this is an opportunity to invest more in the war machine."

‌



The Foreign Secretary also issued a warning over the Kremlin's ties to Iran. Speaking on a visit to Saudi Arabia, Ms Cooper said: "We have seen these links between Russia and Iran over an extended period of time.

"We're seeing it in terms of technology, we see it in terms of the approach, we see it in terms of these kinds of tactics, and we see it in terms of the way these two states try to support each other and try to benefit together from attempting to hijack the global economy."

She added: "We are very clear about the threat from both Russia and from Iran to the global economy and to all of our wellbeing."

Article continues below

It comes as RAF jets widened their patrol of the skies to Bahrain on Thursday night to hunt Iranian drones.

Downing Street said: "Overnight, UK jets flew air defence sorties specifically in defence of Bahrain for the first time. This saw Typhoon jets patrolling the skies for Iranian drones heading towards Bahrain.

"British Typhoon and F35 jets are now flying in defence of British people, bases and our partners, moving in Qatar, Cyprus, UAE, Jordan and Bahrain."

News.title: King Charles hails groundbreaking sustainable innovations at global summit
News.content:
King Charles hails groundbreaking sustainable innovations at global summit

The King has been joined by more than 300 of the world's leading CEOs to discuss sustainable initiatives that will help fight climate change

View 4 Images King Charles founded the sustainable initiative five years ago (Image: AP)

King Charles attended the conclusion of the second Annual Sustainable Markets Initiative Exhibition (SMI) and Reception, at Hampton Court Palace, after a two-day summit with global industry and finance leaders and government representatives.

‌



The monarch marvelled at exhibits of innovations developed by industries, including sustainable textiles, carbon capture models and an electric aeroplane. Charles was so engrossed that the tour of the exhibit overran, as he was heard saying “fantastic” and “magnificent” several times. The King appeared wowed by a scale model of a machine that will clear up space debris, and marvelled at a replica of the Blue Origin space shuttle - which famously fired Katy Perry and Gayle King into orbit.

‌



View 4 Images King Charles attended the second Annual Sustainable Markets Initiative Exhibition (Image: PA)

‌



At the event, Charles was shown a model of the space debris removal satellite, called Elsa-M, which has been designed and built by Astroscale UK. Nick Shave, UK managing director of the company, said that the King had previously visited its site at Harwell Science and Innovation Campus in Oxfordshire.

Mr Shave said: “He remembered visiting us four years ago so I told him how much we developed on. He was pleased to see we’re making progress – he seemed very interested.”

The King later joined CEOs, 50 of whom were from China, to discuss what they have achieved during the two-day conference. He then met more guests in The Haunted Gallery and early shook hands with former US Secretary of State John Kerry.

‌



View 4 Images The monarch viewed a replica of Blue Origin's New Shepard, fully reusable suborbital rocket system (Image: PA)

The King launched the SMI to encourage private companies to invest in sustainability in 2020. The Sustainable Markets Initiative’s Roundtables and Exhibition was launched last year to showcase private investment firms and initiatives.

Sustainable Markets Initiative, CEO Jen Jordan-Saifi said: "We were absolutely delighted that His Majesty joined us today to see first-hand the remarkable support behind the Sustainable Markets Initiative and the exhibition showcasing the work of our member companies.

‌



"His Majesty founded the SMI with a clear vision - that the private sector must play a decisive role in delivering the sustainable transition.

View 4 Images Charles viewed models of ships, including the World's first ammonia fuelled shipping vessel (Image: PA)

Article continues below

"What we are seeing at this Summit is that vision in action, with CEOs from around the world coming together to deliver practical solutions and real progress”.

Former Secretary of State John Kerry has joined the Ocean Stewardship Initiative (OSI) as a Champion. Earlier, Charles also convened the Fashion Task Force at Buckingham Palace to discuss major progress across flagship projects advancing regenerative fashion and traceability through Digital Product Passports (DPP).

Boxing.title: Jake Paul suffers embarrassing hygiene gaffe during Donald Trump rally appearance as major wardrobe error exposes long-held insecurity
Boxing.content:
YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump at a political rally.

Paul joined Trump on stage at Verst Logistics in Kentucky and was given the chance to address the crowd.

The 29-year-old went viral on social media, not for his comments about Trump and American manufacturing, but instead about his appearance.

Many pointed out vast sweat patches that were visible on Paul's beige suit as he spoke to the crowd.

The patches were particularly prominent under his armpits, which were exposed further when he raised his arms to the crowd.

Paul had previously spoken about how sweating had long been a major insecurity of his.

Jake Paul gets on stage at the Trump rally and has the nastiest pit stains I've ever seen



(it also appears the crowd doesn't know who he is) pic.twitter.com/3Yc071tZzf — Aaron Rupar (@atrupar) March 11, 2026

YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump

Paul and his brother Logan had promoted his deodorant brand ahead of his fight with Mike Tyson back in 2024

Paul made the revelation back in 2024 when he launched a deodorant brand W, which he and his brother Logan promoted before and after his bout with Mike Tyson.

'Sweating has been a longtime insecurity of mine,' Paul had said when launching the brand.

'If you know me, you know I'm a clean freak. I always want to smell fresh, but training in Puerto Rico's humid climate makes it a real challenge.

'I needed a product that could keep up with my lifestyle — one that smells amazing but isn't full of junk ingredients, and honestly, one that wasn't from 2006.

'That's why I started W… to create products for guys that will boost their confidence and smell amazing, but also don't cost $20 for a stick of deodorant.'

The W deodorants are claimed to offer 24 hours protection, with the brand website stating: 'With doubled scent load, increased hydration and more sweat-blocking power, our new deodorant and body wash are a monumental breakthrough for mankind (or at least for your morning)'.

During the event, Trump signalled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

Join the debate Did YOU spot Jake Paul's hygiene gaffe?

Trump had brought Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday in Kentucky

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

'And you have my complete and total endorsement.'

Paul had expressed his admiration for Trump credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul added. And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us.

'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'



The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavours in the boxing ring over recent years.

Boxing.title: Donald Trump gives Jake Paul shock endorsement for political career as boxer joins President at Kentucky rally
Boxing.content:
President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday and made a bold prediction about the social media star's future.

The 29-year-old Ohio native, who has transitioned from a YouTube personality to a major force in the combat sports world, stood alongside the president as the crowd in Hebron, Ky roared in support.

During the event, Trump signaled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

The president didn't stop at a mere prediction, immediately offering his political backing to the 'Problem Child' should he decide to pursue a seat in the future.

'And you have my complete and total endorsement,' the president added, cementing a public alliance with one of the most polarizing and popular figures in modern media.

President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience

Addressing the Kentucky crowd, Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

He told the crowd that the president had taught him the value of 'courage' and to 'never back down from a fight,' a mantra Paul has lived by throughout his boxing career.
